ops.title: Another live session's uncommitted WIP can appear mid-flight in this shared checkout — triage file ownership before commits and before \"fixing pre-existing\" review findings
ops.body:
cc-notes is often edited by two live sessions sharing one working copy (2026-07-16: an MCP-parity session's files appeared between my baseline `jj st` and ship time). Two traps:

**Why:** review finders report on the whole tree, so a "pre-existing bug" may live in a file the other session hasn't committed yet (check `git cat-file -e @-:path` — absent means it's their WIP; don't silently patch it, comment on their durable task instead). And a whole-file pathspec commit sweeps their hunks when both sessions touched one file.

**How to apply:** before shipping, re-run `jj st` and diff-stat every file against your known change set; for an interleaved file, reconstruct a mine-only version (parent + my hunks), `jj commit <explicit filesets>` from repo root, restore the combined file right after — jj then rebases their @ onto the new commit cleanly. Verify the commit in isolation via `git archive <sha> | tar -x -C tmpdir` + build/test there, since the working tree can't prove the commit compiles. Related: [[jj-commit-snapshots-whole-worktree]], [[verify-lineage-before-ship]].
